examples: It was named after Amerigo vespucci who landed in America in the late fifteenth century.
However, it was another Spanish explorer, Amerigo Vespucci who gave his name to the new continent.
When they described hostile natives as friendly traders, Vespucci and his fellow explorers served their vested interests.
Thomas Jefferson hung portraits of Columbus and Vespucci at Monticello, his Virginia home.
Mr Winchester is good on stories about heavyweight Atlantic names: Columbus, Vespucci, Nelson.
